114986.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in Haarlem. Man, woman, non-binary. In such a government all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

114987.explanation: When you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, wonderful things emerge. The municipality should not stop this but rather encourage it.

114989.explanation: A lively Haarlem belongs with a lively nightlife. Especially cafes contribute to this. For young people and creative makers, the night is often the stage to develop themselves. We find it important that they have a place in the city.

114990.explanation: There is room in Haarlem to shelter refugees and asylum seekers. Newcomers deserve a fair start in Haarlem. We offer language support and ensure that people are employed.

114991.explanation: In Haarlem, various organizations like 'Stem in de Stad' are active in supporting rejected asylum seekers. We find it important that they can continue doing this. It is undesirable that this is prohibited.

114993.explanation: Wood burning pollutes the air and harms the health of Haarlemmers. That is why we first focus on awareness and tools such as the Stookwijzer. If that is insufficient, we look at a temporary burning ban on days with little or no wind.

114994.explanation: Patio heating cannot just be banned. That is why we choose to work with horeca entrepreneurs to minimize patio heating as much as possible and offer more sustainable forms.

114995.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all enjoy together, with one large fireworks show organized by the municipality. This way, we keep it safe and can all enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

114998.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

115000.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in Haarlem, from the street to the city council. But many local media struggle to stay afloat. We believe the municipality should contribute to this.

115003.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

115006.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

115007.explanation: Since 2024, enforcers in Haarlem may use a baton if there is a threat of violence. As far as we are concerned, they should be allowed to continue doing this.

115008.explanation: Haarlem is building many new social rental homes. We find it fairer if you get priority for these homes if you have an economic or social bond with the city.

115011.explanation: Haarlem has already purchased container homes for the temporary housing of Ukrainians near Haarlem Spaarnwoude station. If these are no longer needed for shelter, they will be available to young people and students. We also want to add extra (temporary) housing for students in other places.

115012.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

115013.explanation: If the remission of municipal taxes stops, Haarlemmers with the lowest incomes will get into serious trouble. They simply cannot do without that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water; that is why we support remission.

115014.explanation: We find it important that Haarlemmers with the lowest incomes are helped. Because many things are getting more expensive, we have already raised the income threshold. We think it is good if this threshold is raised further, for example to enable children to participate in sports.

115017.explanation: More and more electric cars drive around Haarlem, which are for example shared by residents. The number of charging points must grow along with this increase.

115021.explanation: Good care is very important for everyone. In a free country, you choose yourself who helps you when you are ill. The government or insurer must not decide that for you. Making your own choices also means receiving help from someone who understands your faith or culture. Then you feel safer.

115024.explanation: Dogs use public spaces, for example dog parks, and there is nuisance from dog poop on streets. The municipality needs money to keep these places clean and well-maintained. It is fair that dog owners contribute to this, instead of all residents.

115026.explanation: The Waarderpolder is our employment engine. We like that new companies are coming. There is enough space for new companies and new jobs in the southern part of the Waarderpolder.

115031.explanation: If an entrepreneur wants to open a new coffeeshop, this is allowed as long as he complies with the rules and the neighborhood does not experience nuisance. This way we ensure that people do not buy soft drugs on the street. That causes nuisance and is unsafe.

115032.explanation: At the moment, the waiting lists for a home are way too long and Haarlemmers with a low income can hardly find anywhere to live. By building more social rental homes, starters and families with less money get a fair chance to get a home.

115035.explanation: A community center is an important place where people of all ages can meet. This helps against loneliness and creates a strong bond in the neighborhood. Residents can also go there for help or fun activities. Such a place makes the neighborhood more pleasant and social for everyone.

115037.explanation: More asphalt means less space for greenery, trees and play areas for children. Extra parking spots actually attract more cars, which leads to more congestion and unsafe streets.

115038.explanation: If we expand the car-free area, pedestrians and cyclists will get a lot more space. This is not only safer but also more pleasant for shops and terraces. Moreover, the air in the city will become cleaner. This way, we make Haarlem's city center a nice place for everyone.

115039.explanation: If we expand the car-free area, pedestrians and cyclists will get a lot more space. This is not only safer but also more pleasant for shops and terraces. Moreover, the air in the city will become cleaner. This way, we make Haarlem's city center a nice place for everyone.

115040.explanation: Fully sustainable heating networks are necessary to achieve our climate goals and to be gas-free by 2040 at the latest. During the transition to a gas-free city in 2040, occasional temporary use of gas will be needed to ensure affordability and supply security.

115041.explanation: If parking becomes free again, parking problems and congestion will immediately return. We don't want that, because then there will be less space on the street for greenery, pedestrians, and cyclists.

114986.explanation: D66 wants the best people in the best positions. Although we strive for a balanced distribution and as diverse a board as possible, we do not commit to an exact 50/50 split; a female majority, for example, is also perfectly fine.

114987.explanation: D66 is enthusiastic about Haarlemmers who want to commit themselves to carrying out a municipal task themselves. In recent years, beautiful initiatives have emerged in Haarlem and this is already happening. That is why D66 wants to retain the so-called 'right to challenge.'

114989.explanation: D66 stands for a vibrant and livable city. One closing time for all cafés is not necessarily good for the city's livability. Then all hospitality guests are on the street simultaneously, which can cause nuisance. Therefore, D66 believes that opening times may vary per café.

114990.explanation: D66 Haarlem wants to offer people fleeing war and violence a safe place. Closing reception locations in Haarlem, for example, causes families to have to sleep on the grass in Ter Apel. We believe this is unacceptable.

114991.explanation: D66 Haarlem wants to offer people fleeing war and violence a safe place. This can only happen if rejected asylum seekers leave our shelters and return. In the meantime, we offer them emergency aid to ensure they do not end up on the streets and cause nuisance.

114993.explanation: D66 wants to reduce health damage caused by wood burning. Wood burning emits a lot of fine particulate matter. Through the use of a burning guide, education, and filters, we want to limit harmful emissions. A ban is only considered if these measures prove insufficient.

114994.explanation: D66 is fully committed to making our energy system sustainable but is cautious about imposing bans. With green energy and encouraging alternatives, such as electric heated blankets, a ban on terrace heaters is not necessary.

114995.explanation: D66 is pleased that a nationwide fireworks ban is coming. The annual number of victims is no longer justifiable, and the police have been calling for this for years. The national fireworks ban may allow exceptions for local fireworks displays. D66 is positive about this.

114998.explanation: For D66, accessibility is a right that makes our city freer for seniors and people with disabilities. We also invest in this by removing thresholds and opening toilets, among other things. The municipality also encourages entrepreneurs to do so, but not with subsidies.

115000.explanation: D66 believes local media should largely secure their own income. Local media do have an important social function: they keep the municipality alert. Through the Haarlem Media Fund, the municipality already supports local media with a small contribution. D66 supports this.

115003.explanation: D66 believes healthy sports clubs are crucial and the municipality already supports them extensively. However, clubs also have their own responsibility for their finances. We encourage self-reliance over permanent emergency support so that taxpayer money is spent responsibly on sports.

115006.explanation: D66 believes safety is about people, not just technology. We are cautious about adding more cameras because they invade privacy and often only shift nuisance elsewhere. D66 prefers more neighborhood police officers and enforcement officers on the streets; real eyes that work preventively and can intervene immediately.

115007.explanation: Currently, only enforcement officers in Haarlem with specific qualifications and tasks carry a baton. Experience shows that the baton is rarely used but greatly improves the sense of security for enforcement officers. Therefore, D66 supports the current policy.

115008.explanation: D66 believes it is important that people who live in Haarlem, work here, or have ties to the city can find affordable housing nearby. Because the housing market does not stop at the municipal boundary, we work with municipalities in the region to distribute social rental homes.

115011.explanation: D66 believes students bring energy and economic strength to the city. To quickly realize housing, D66 wants to invest in student housing and possibly flexible housing.

115012.explanation: D66 wants everyone on welfare benefits to participate. However, a compulsory counter-performance is not reasonable: some people cannot work because they are ill, others need extra support. D66 wants to assess per person what is needed to get people back on track and into work.

115013.explanation: Residents who truly do not have enough money to pay municipal taxes currently do not have to pay. D66 supports this. If we did not do this, we would push these residents further into debt. In the long term, this would cost us much more money.

115014.explanation: D66 wants to support people with low incomes but also wants work to pay off. Therefore, we want to phase out local income schemes more gradually as income rises. This helps people far more than continuously raising the income limit.

115017.explanation: D66 wants the transition to electric driving to be easy for everyone. More charging points are also essential for clean air and a healthy city.

115021.explanation: D66 believes it is important that high-quality care is accessible to everyone. This is our priority and is a major challenge due to staff shortages. Choosing a healthcare provider based on specific individual preferences will therefore not always be possible or necessary.

115024.explanation: D66 finds it important that there are special facilities for dogs and their owners. Think of dog parks. These facilities cost the municipality money, and D66 therefore finds it reasonable that dog owners contribute via dog tax.

115026.explanation: Entrepreneurship is one of the engines behind Haarlem's success. Wherever possible, D66 therefore wants to allow limited expansion of business parks. Green areas or attractive residential locations are not eligible as expansion areas for business parks.

115031.explanation: D66 supports a regulated drug policy. This ensures responsible use and prevents nuisance. D66 is therefore not automatically opposed to a new coffee shop as long as it is not located near schools and does not cause nuisance.

115032.explanation: D66 wants balanced, mixed neighborhoods. By not focusing solely on social housing, we also make room for mid-rent housing. We aim for 30% to 50% social housing per neighborhood to maintain balance. Our ambition is that a total of 80% of all new buildings is intended for residents with low or middle incomes.

115035.explanation: D66 wishes everyone a neighborhood where residents know and help each other. Places where residents can meet are crucial for this. D66 wants to offer everyone this possibility through community centers, although some need it more than others.

115037.explanation: D66 does want to build new parking garages to reduce parking pressure on the streets. D66 believes we must return the street to residents. For every spot in a garage, a spot on the street for greenery or play disappears.

115038.explanation: D66 wants more car-reduced zones and car-free residential concepts. In the city center, residents who already have a car should, for example, be able to continue parking in parking garages. By giving priority to pedestrians and cyclists, we make the center more livable.

115039.explanation: D66 wants to return the beautiful quays of the Spaarne to pedestrians and residents. By making this area low-traffic, space for greenery and social interaction arises instead of through traffic.

115040.explanation: D66 wants Haarlem to be completely gas-free by 2040. We are therefore fully committed to clean heating networks. By phasing out fossil fuels, we protect our climate and ensure an energy supply that is independent, affordable, and future-proof.

115041.explanation: D66 believes regulated parking should be cost-covering. This effectively tackles parking pressure without burdening the city financially. By regulating, we prevent nuisance from shifting to other neighborhoods and free up space on the streets for greenery, play, and additional housing.

114986.explanation: Diversity is important. At the same time, the mayor and aldermen must perform their duties well. Quality must not be forgotten. It is good to strive for as much diversity as possible with an eye to maintaining quality.

114987.explanation: We want to minimize litter across the entire city and address delayed maintenance of sidewalks and streets in neighborhoods. We will make new agreements with Spaarnelanden about this. Where there are initiatives from residents, VVD Haarlem wants to support them.

114989.explanation: Where livability and safety can be sufficiently guaranteed, we will extend the opening hours of hospitality venues.

114990.explanation: As required by the dispersal law, we provide reception for refugees. We organize meaningful daytime activities with the COA, in cooperation with entrepreneurs, organizations, and institutions. We enforce safety near the shelter with cameras and security guards.

114991.explanation: Haarlem is obliged to accommodate asylum seekers. Once they are rejected and their procedures are complete, they no longer have a valid right to live in the Netherlands. There is then no longer a municipal task to accommodate them.

114993.explanation: We find it important that residents can choose how to heat their homes themselves. With good information, the municipality can raise awareness about responsible heating to minimize nuisance for people with respiratory problems and the environment as much as possible.

114994.explanation: We strive for less regulatory pressure and more space for entrepreneurs to do business. We do not think a ban on terrace heating fits a vibrant city. In addition, more energy-efficient alternatives are becoming available on the market.

114995.explanation: From 2026 onwards, there will be a nationwide fireworks ban for residents. Fireworks have long been a tradition on New Year's Eve. A municipal fireworks show maintains this tradition and also fosters connection.

114998.explanation: VVD Haarlem recognizes the societal importance of accessibility and believes that the municipality can contribute to keeping facilities easily accessible for everyone through supportive measures.

115000.explanation: For reliable, independent local reporting, we want to financially better support regional journalism.

115003.explanation: We acknowledge the great societal importance of financially healthy sports clubs and associations. Therefore, according to VVD Haarlem, the municipality should continue to keep sports and exercise facilities easily accessible to everyone through supportive measures.

115006.explanation: For VVD Haarlem, the feeling of safety for residents is a top priority. Camera surveillance is an effective tool to improve safety in public spaces. We advocate for a balance between safety and privacy in this regard.

115007.explanation: VVD Haarlem has launched a pilot in which enforcement officers can use a short baton, with success. We believe this practice should continue.

115008.explanation: We are investigating how to prioritize housing for people in essential professions such as teachers, healthcare workers, firefighters, police, and for children who have lived at home for a long time.

115011.explanation: VVD Haarlem advocates for sufficient housing for every target group in Haarlem. Students also deserve a place to live. To meet the high housing demand, VVD also looks at creative additional measures to enable more square meters.

115012.explanation: The principle is that everyone contributes to society according to their ability. For people receiving social assistance benefits, this means that, where possible, they do socially meaningful work or participate through a tailored workplace or suitable daily activities.

115013.explanation: VVD Haarlem helps those who need support and guides them towards independence. Low-income individuals have access to a broad financial and social safety net, which includes the cancellation of municipal taxes.

115014.explanation: VVD Haarlem helps those who need support and guides them towards independence. Low-income individuals have access to a broad financial and social safety net, especially if they make use of all existing arrangements. According to VVD, no more is necessary.

115017.explanation: Electric driving requires an increasingly extensive network of charging facilities. That is why VVD Haarlem wants to work with market parties in the coming years to increase the number of charging stations on streets and in business parks.

115021.explanation: Although VVD Haarlem values freedom of choice in healthcare, resources are limited to provide sufficient care. Therefore, unlimited freedom of choice is not possible. The supply must be proven effective and diverse.

115024.explanation: VVD Haarlem chooses financial peace of mind with low local taxes. The dog tax offers no direct benefits to dog owners, such as facilities or bags, and is mainly an extra levy. That is unfair.

115026.explanation: We cherish the Waarderpolder. We maintain the development of the South strip there. We do not want housing construction in the Waarderpolder. That would come at the expense of business space. We are careful with land designated for offices, businesses, hospitality, and services throughout the city.

115031.explanation: Undermining of public administration and drug crime by criminal organizations is a major problem. VVD Haarlem wants the police and municipality to continue addressing the disruptive nature of this crime. This must be considered when establishing a coffeeshop.

115032.explanation: Building more homes is a priority. If projects do not exactly meet the municipal requirement to build 40% social rental, 40% mid-segment, and 20% free-sector housing, we look at what is feasible. We especially aim to build additional homes for middle incomes.

115035.explanation: Strengthening the neighborhood feeling and looking out for each other more often. We want to encourage that again. Just caring a bit more for each other. We want to do this together with you and all the professionals in your neighborhood, sports club, community center, or school.

115037.explanation: The number of cars will continue to grow in the coming years. Therefore, VVD Haarlem advocates for more and better parking facilities for cars throughout the city, whether or not in the form of parking garages.

115038.explanation: VVD Haarlem believes the city center must be accessible and vibrant. However, we want to build extra parking facilities around the old city center so that people can continue to come to the city by car.

115039.explanation: VVD Haarlem believes the city center must be accessible and vibrant. However, we want to build extra parking facilities around the old city center so that people can continue to come to the city by car.

115040.explanation: Together with Liander, we look at how to increase the electricity grid capacity & the municipality continues investing in heat networks and other energy sources. We explore with residents per neighborhood the possibilities to phase out natural gas: through heat pumps or a heat network.

115041.explanation: According to VVD, paid parking should only be introduced if a large majority of the neighborhood agrees. There must be sufficient support. In some neighborhoods where paid parking has now been introduced, that support is lacking. It is therefore conceivable to evaluate this decision.

114986.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes the municipal executive should be as inclusive and diverse as possible and reflective of society. This is much broader than the binary male-female ratio.

114987.explanation: There is already experience with this in Haarlem (Right to Challenge). The Party for the Animals would like residents to be actively involved together in their environment. This contributes to pleasant green neighborhoods. The municipality should support such initiatives.

114989.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants a lively and socially safe nightlife culture in Haarlem. But balancing liveliness and livability is also important. With clear rules on opening hours, both entrepreneurs and residents know what to expect.

114990.explanation: Haarlem has traditionally been a welcoming city. We help people fleeing war and violence. With good guidance from, for example, labor coaches, language education and Refugee Work, they can participate fully in society.

114991.explanation: The shelter and guidance of people without documents must be well arranged. People without a residence permit receive access to day and night shelters and are legally and socially assisted.

114993.explanation: The PvdD wants healthy air for everyone. Wood burning causes a lot of fine dust and damages the health of many in Haarlem.

114994.explanation: The climate crisis is now. Therefore, we focus on energy savings instead of energy waste. Unfortunately, the municipality has no authority to ban terrace heaters, which is a matter for the national government. Haarlem lobbies the national government and offers subsidies itself for, for example, heat cushions.

114995.explanation: Festive, safe, and environmentally friendly alternatives to fireworks, such as shows with light, music, drones, and lasers, can take place as long as people, animals, nature, and the environment do not suffer any harm.

114998.explanation: Haarlem wants to be an accessible city for everyone and therefore has an Accessibility Coordinator. This advisor helps entrepreneurs make their buildings accessible. The costs are borne by the entrepreneurs themselves.

115000.explanation: Strong, independent local media and regional broadcasters are very important to the residents of Haarlem. To promote independent (investigative) journalism, a widely accessible local media fund will be established for non-commercial local media.

115003.explanation: Sports are healthy, sports are fun, and sports foster more connections between people. The municipality can help ensure sports are accessible and safe for everyone.

115006.explanation: Regarding camera surveillance in public spaces, the Party for the Animals wants to be very cautious for privacy reasons. Camera surveillance offers only apparent safety and often merely shifts the problem elsewhere.

115007.explanation: The baton is a means of violence. The Party for the Animals wants the municipality of Haarlem to reverse the decision to equip enforcement officers with it.

115008.explanation: Current social rental rules give first priority to people with an urgency declaration or a vital profession such as teachers and nurses. Then priority goes to residents of Haarlem and people from the region.

115011.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes the municipality must invest in permanent sustainable housing for students. We want students to be able to live affordably.

115012.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants the municipality of Haarlem to put the resident and their needs at the center and have a broader and more generous view of social assistance recipients. We do not ask for a reciprocal obligation.

115013.explanation: Haarlem applies the minimum policy for incomes up to 130% of the social minimum. Poverty is combated through good, prevention-focused debt counseling. Combating child poverty has priority in Haarlem.

115014.explanation: Haarlem applies the minimum policy for incomes up to 130% of the social minimum. Poverty is combated through good, prevention-focused debt counseling. Combating child poverty has priority in Haarlem.

115017.explanation: The municipality must ensure sufficient charging points with green electricity. We seek smart solutions, for example with charging points in the ground or in lampposts. This keeps public spaces accessible. We also focus on using car batteries as batteries.

115021.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to prevent discrimination, for example when a person refuses care from another person because of, for instance, their gender, sexual orientation, belief, or religion.

115024.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants no taxes to be levied on any animal. Animals are not a source of profit.

115026.explanation: Business parks that no longer have a future or cause too much disturbance to surrounding residential areas will be transformed into circular spaces, residential neighborhoods, and greenery. When restructuring business parks that do have a future perspective, space will be used much more efficiently.

115031.explanation: Haarlem is open to trials with local cannabis cultivation. This cultivation is sustainable and organic. No new coffeeshops will be located near schools.

115032.explanation: By building more social rental homes, more affordable housing becomes available. This increases the proportion of social rental in the total housing stock.

115035.explanation: Social cohesion between residents and connection within the neighborhood are very important for the health and safety of the people of Haarlem. Community centers play a very important role in this.

115037.explanation: Parked cars in Haarlem occupy space equivalent to more than 100 football fields. By focusing more on walking, cycling, public transport, and shared cars, the space currently taken up by parking spots becomes available. This allows us to plant many new trees and create more room for play and social interaction.

115038.explanation: A car-free city center is nicer for everyone and offers opportunities for further greening. Residents can use existing parking garages, and visitors can come by bike or public transport. Disabled persons can park in the city center.

115039.explanation: A car-free city center is nicer for everyone and offers opportunities for further greening and the development of the Spaarneoever Park. Residents can use existing parking garages, and visitors come by bike or public transport. Disabled persons can park in the city center.

115040.explanation: District heating networks are only fully sustainable if they do not require gas or other fossil fuels as support. Additionally, the Party for the Animals believes that energy generation involving animals (manure digesters, burning carcasses or slaughter waste) must be eliminated.

115041.explanation: Cars take up a lot of space. This comes at the expense of greenery, accessible sidewalks, and space for playing and socializing. By implementing permit parking, we ensure that the number of cars in Haarlem does not increase. Parked cars in Haarlem already occupy more than 100 football fields worth of space.

114986.explanation: The CDA considers equal opportunities for women and men in governance important. However, this statement assumes a fixed outcome (50/50), while the composition of a council depends on coalitions, available candidates, and quality, which is our starting point.

114987.explanation: The CDA believes in taking responsibility together and in the power of involved residents. Where residents want and can manage, for example, a sports hall or park themselves, this should be possible.

114989.explanation: The CDA believes hospitality is important for social interaction, enjoyment, and a lively city. At the same time, residents have the right to peace, safety, and livability. Completely free opening hours do not do justice to this balance. We advocate for tailor-made solutions – see, for example, the Patronaat where it works.

114990.explanation: The CDA chooses humanity and responsibility. Asylum reception is not a temporary switch that you can just turn off. We stand for well-organized reception, fair distribution, and community support, with attention to safety and livability.

114991.explanation: The reception and return of rejected asylum seekers is primarily the responsibility of the national government. The CDA believes municipalities should not structurally take over tasks that must be arranged nationally, but this should be done with regard for human dignity and public order.

114993.explanation: In densely built-up neighborhoods, heating with open fireplaces and wood stoves can cause nuisance to neighbors. The CDA chooses livability and good relations in the neighborhood and is therefore cautious about allowing this.

114994.explanation: The CDA wants to save energy and encourage sustainability but does not believe a general ban is the right measure. We choose clear rules, conscious use, and stimulating sustainable alternatives, with attention to entrepreneurs and livability.

114995.explanation: A centralized fireworks show contributes to social interaction and community feeling. It is a festive moment for young and old and a safe alternative to personal fireworks. This way, we celebrate New Year's together, in a way that connects people.

114998.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in Haarlem. The CDA therefore supports entrepreneurs who want to make their premises more accessible. With targeted subsidies, we help make this possible, sharing responsibility so not everything falls on the municipality.

115000.explanation: The local broadcaster is important for the city. Extra structural funding is not a given for the CDA; we must handle community funds carefully and make good use of existing resources.

115003.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for health and cohesion. Every neighborhood deserves a vital club. If clubs temporarily face difficulties, the municipality can help, targeted and temporary, so they can grow independently again.

115006.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety and preventing nuisance. The CDA supports targeted and careful camera surveillance in places where it is needed, respecting privacy and with clear rules.

115007.explanation: Borough enforcement officers play an important role in street safety. Due to increased aggression and violence against officers, it is necessary that they can protect themselves. The CDA therefore believes the baton can remain in Haarlem.

115008.explanation: The CDA finds it important that people who live in Haarlem or have a strong connection to it can also continue living in their own city. Priority for home seekers from the municipality contributes to livable neighborhoods and social cohesion.

115011.explanation: The housing shortage requires practical solutions. Temporary student housing can help relieve pressure. This is already happening in Haarlem, among others near the station. The CDA supports this approach.

115012.explanation: Participation is important. The CDA believes that people with welfare benefits, where possible, can do socially meaningful work. This way they contribute to the community and remain involved in society.

115013.explanation: Forgiving debts prevents people with low incomes from unnecessarily falling into debt. The CDA believes this helps residents participate in society. Debt problems ultimately cost society much more.

115014.explanation: The CDA believes people who work and still struggle to make ends meet should be able to receive support. Haarlem is already raising this income threshold. This ensures that working pays off and everyone can participate in the community.

115017.explanation: Sustainability and clean mobility are important for Haarlem. The CDA supports expanding charging points for electric cars. Sufficient charging spaces are needed to enable residents to switch to electric driving.

115021.explanation: The choice of care provider primarily belongs to the healthcare system and insurers. The municipality does not control this. The CDA does believe care must be respectful and appropriate, but this cannot be enforced through municipal policy.

115024.explanation: Dogs bring people together and help reduce loneliness. The CDA therefore supports abolishing the dog tax. This keeps dog ownership accessible and strengthens the community spirit in neighborhoods.

115026.explanation: Haarlem is a city to live and work in. The CDA supports expanding business parks while preserving the Waarderpolder as a high-quality industrial area. This maintains space for entrepreneurship, craftsmanship, and sustainable employment.

115031.explanation: Haarlem has a careful coffeeshop policy and currently there are enough coffeeshops in the city. The CDA sees no need for new locations and wants to maintain the current balance.

115032.explanation: The CDA opts for a feasible and balanced housing mix. In new construction, we aim for at least 40% social rental or purchase and at least 40% mid-range. This ensures flow and affordable housing for different groups.

115035.explanation: Every neighborhood deserves a place for meeting and activities. That does not always have to be a new community center. The CDA focuses on strong clubs and existing facilities, with good support and affordable space, so community life can grow.

115037.explanation: Parking requires tailor-made solutions per neighborhood. The CDA chooses a careful balance between parking pressure and livability. More parking spaces often come at the expense of green and play areas; therefore, we assess what is needed and possible per neighborhood.

115038.explanation: For many residents, the car is indispensable. The CDA is therefore against a completely car-free center. In neighborhoods with high parking pressure, no spaces will be lost, and on the city outskirts, we provide Park & Ride facilities so visitors can reach the center without a car.

115039.explanation: The CDA finds accessibility important for residents, entrepreneurs, and emergency services. A low-car western side of the Spaarne at this location could pressure accessibility. We choose a good balance between livability and accessibility.

115040.explanation: The CDA stands for a sustainable energy transition. In Haarlem, we only work with sustainable district heating networks; fossil fuel networks do not exist. We therefore support that new district heating networks are fully sustainable.

115041.explanation: Paid parking makes it easier for residents to park in their own neighborhood again. The CDA finds it important that parking pressure is regulated and that neighborhoods remain accessible and livable.

114986.explanation: For OPHaarlem it is not a must, but an ambition to have as many women as men in the College of Mayor and Aldermen. Our mayor is a man, which with the current number of aldermen means three women and two men. Currently, it is the opposite. We make this a point of attention.

114987.explanation: A sports hall is of a completely different scale than a public park. The very complex management of many sports halls, swimming pools, and gymnasiums is carried out by SRO. OPHaarlem considers 'adopting' a park a good initiative to promote residents' involvement with their neighborhood or district.

114989.explanation: It should be the aim of our municipality and the hospitality sector to set opening hours in good consultation. That is OPHaarlem’s starting point. The municipality always has a role in preventing nuisance, which can mean restricting the opening hours of cafes, restaurants, and clubs.

114990.explanation: Asylum reception is a national and international legal obligation. OPHaarlem neither can nor wants to shirk that responsibility.

114991.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers who are not returned by our government to their own or another country and wander homeless here must be able to receive shelter. If this does not happen, the municipality is effectively 'forced' to arrange it. We support that.

114993.explanation: OPHaarlem believes we should raise awareness of the Stove Guide with a good campaign for Haarlem residents. If owners of fireplaces and wood stoves follow it well, nuisance will be greatly reduced for people sensitive to wood smoke.

114994.explanation: OPHaarlem thinks terrace heaters should preferably not be used and, in any case, used with great restraint, but we balance this against the enormous energy consumption of a data center and therefore cannot explain a ban.

114995.explanation: OPHaarlem completely agrees with this statement, and our Spaarne seems an ideal place for a wonderful New Year's Eve fireworks show.

114998.explanation: In 2024, the municipality of Haarlem has made 34 buildings accessible to people with disabilities. Entrepreneurs have a social responsibility to do this themselves, but if really needed, the municipality should assist with knowledge and additional financial support.

115000.explanation: Our local broadcaster is already receiving substantially more temporary subsidy. There is great attention for the continuity of Haarlem105. OPHaarlem wants to keep our local broadcaster operational.

115003.explanation: Acute bottlenecks must be resolved quickly where at all possible. Subsequently, the causes must be investigated together with the club(s). Sometimes a merger with another club can be a solution, or updating the offer, so we agree but with a caveat.

115006.explanation: For OPHaarlem, it is important that there is adequate camera surveillance at risky locations and that this is arranged in consultation with the municipal council. We are not primarily concerned with more surveillance but with targeted and effective supervision.

115007.explanation: Haarlem is running a baton trial until mid-year. During this trial period, BOAs have shown the baton in 6 cases and used it in 4 cases. OPHaarlem wants to wait for the final evaluation and discuss it in the municipal council before taking a position.

115008.explanation: In December, a new housing regulation was adopted; with this, up to 50% priority can now be given to Haarlem residents. It is important that the construction volume remains high. If the social housing waiting list does not decrease, more priority must be given.

115011.explanation: Being a student city comes with obligations. Our students must be able to find housing in Haarlem. Our housing corporations must step up in the coming years to realize more student homes. If temporary container homes are needed, they must finance them.

115012.explanation: Having to do social work is not a good basis for it. OPHaarlem knows many people on welfare who are committed to their loved ones and others around them. It can be useful to inform people on welfare about volunteering.

115013.explanation: Waiver of municipal taxes can only be granted to Haarlem residents with a low income, and OPHaarlem considers that a good thing. The waiver request must be submitted to Cocensus.

115014.explanation: For the Haarlem Pass, our municipality applies an income limit of 130% of the social assistance norm. OPHaarlem has advocated for this. Besides the statutory social assistance standards, Haarlem also has special social assistance. An energy allowance has been granted for several years, and we want it reinstated.

115017.explanation: A memo on this topic will come to the municipal council this spring. The current grid congestion is a limiting factor mentioned in the memo. The expansion of charging points will require some balancing in the coming years, but the number will grow, which OPHaarlem considers a good thing.

115021.explanation: OPHaarlem believes there should always be a match between care recipient and care provider. Where possible, it is good to meet specific wishes of care recipients, but it cannot be guaranteed because care providers are very hard to find.

115024.explanation: Does the municipality incur costs for dogs? At least for placing trash bins and maintaining them. Abolishing dog tax means another tax must be raised. OPHaarlem will continue to gauge public opinion in the city.

115026.explanation: OPHaarlem believes our industrial estates should in principle not expand. Assuming new and compact production methods and smart building, there should be compact industrial estates in Haarlem. Only if all creativity is insufficient may minimal expansion be permitted.

115031.explanation: In OPHaarlem's view, there are enough coffee shops in Haarlem. If clear signals emerge that a part of the city really misses a coffee shop, we will consider it.

115032.explanation: Haarlem has had a policy for years to allocate 40% of new construction to social housing. In practice, however, existing social rental homes are being demolished or sold, so the total hardly grows. This bothers OPHaarlem because we want real growth in numbers.

115035.explanation: OPHaarlem agrees. Almost all, perhaps all neighborhoods in Haarlem already have their community center, but where a neighborhood experiences a lack of a center, the municipality must provide it.

115037.explanation: OPHaarlem takes the opposite approach. No parking spaces should be removed to increase parking pressure and thereby prepare a neighborhood for paid parking. As long as our public transport network has many limitations, a car (for work and mobility) is indispensable for many.

115038.explanation: People live and work in our center and residents must also be able to receive friends and family. People with disabilities must also be able to access the center; OPHaarlem calls this the human scale. We are not a party of dogmas but of reasonableness.

115039.explanation: The west side of the Spaarne has a good separation between bicycle and motor traffic, and there are very few parking spaces between the Bakenessergracht and the Gedempte Oude Gracht. That fits OPHaarlem’s definition of car-free.

115040.explanation: The efficiency of heat networks is already lower than individual solutions. If, far into the future, the heat networks remain centrally gas-fired, there really are better solutions for our environment.

115041.explanation: OPHaarlem fully agrees! If a neighborhood did not have the opportunity to conduct a support survey to see if the majority is for or against parking regulation, the municipality should not impose paid parking.

114986.explanation: The best aldermen, those who can effect change, should be decisive.

114987.explanation: If they want to. The say and influence of Haarlem residents is our top priority.

114989.explanation: The municipality has the responsibility to also consider potential nuisance.

114990.explanation: The municipality has no influence on the number of asylum applications. It is fair and appropriate that every municipality participates, with wealthy municipalities like Bloemendaal contributing more.

114993.explanation: No ban, but good information must be given about possible nuisance to others. We trust that people will then adjust their behavior.

114994.explanation: The municipality should not interfere with that.

114995.explanation: That is a good alternative (but not a condition) to the national fireworks ban.

115003.explanation: Sports are important and should not become too expensive, remaining accessible to everyone.

115006.explanation: As a supplement to enforcement officers, not a replacement. It works preventively and can help resolve issues. Privacy must be safeguarded.

115007.explanation: It works preventively and they should be able to do their work safely.

115008.explanation: As much as legally permitted. Not only for rentals but also for owner-occupied homes. Everyone should have the right to affordable housing (not just the minimum income groups).

115011.explanation: And that is a good solution for all urgent groups, so that there is no longer any jumping the queue for social housing.

115012.explanation: In this way, this is unfair competition. The benefit is a temporary provision bridging towards real work.

115013.explanation: As long as the minimum wage is too low, we will not leave people out in the cold.

115014.explanation: As long as the minimum wage is too low, the threshold for assistance must be raised to 150%.

115017.explanation: But not too many. Too many parking spaces have already disappeared. So we prefer that those spaces are added instead.

115026.explanation: More homes must be built first.

115031.explanation: There are more than enough of them.

115032.explanation: It is now 40%, which is too low. There is a severe shortage of affordable rental housing. In the past 12 years, there have even been 86 fewer!

115035.explanation: It promotes living together in the neighborhood.

115037.explanation: That is urgently needed and also possible! Too many spaces have already disappeared everywhere.

115038.explanation: No more than now. In the shopping streets in the city center, this is already the case and that is okay!

115040.explanation: If we proceed with that, it must be done collectively and at the municipality's expense.

115041.explanation: We are against paid parking and want it reversed where it is or has been implemented.

114986.explanation: Your Haarlem wants a council that reflects the diversity of Haarlem. Equal representation is important, not only between men and women but especially also in backgrounds and experiences. This creates a government that truly understands the city and can therefore make better decisions.

114987.explanation: Your Haarlem supports residents who are committed to their neighborhood. The management of a park can be done well by residents, with clear agreements and support from the municipality. A sports hall is too complex for that; safety, maintenance and operation require professional management.

114989.explanation: Your Haarlem finds a good balance between vibrancy and livability essential. With tailored solutions and clear agreements, we ensure that entrepreneurship can thrive and that you can simultaneously live comfortably in our beautiful city.

114990.explanation: Your Haarlem believes that closing asylum centers is not a solution. People seeking protection here deserve safe accommodation. The municipality must provide good guidance and quick integration.

114991.explanation: Accommodation for rejected asylum seekers is not a simple yes/no question. Departure is the principle according to the rules, but there are exceptional situations in which return is not possible. In those cases, we do not leave people to fend for themselves and seek humane solutions.

114993.explanation: Your Haarlem understands that people find a fireplace cozy. At the same time, the municipality has a responsibility for public health. That lies in good information and awareness, not in a general ban. This way, we seek a workable balance between coziness and health.

114994.explanation: We must reduce energy waste, but also involve entrepreneurs in this. A general ban on terrace heaters is legally impossible. Therefore, we support the approach where entrepreneurs receive warm cushions when they give up their terrace heaters: that is both sustainable and feasible.

114995.explanation: With the upcoming fireworks ban, Your Haarlem thinks it is a good idea to organize a municipal fireworks show on New Year's Eve. This provides a safe and festive alternative, with attention to safety, the environment, and animal welfare.

114998.explanation: Your Haarlem believes that everyone should be able to participate. Therefore, the municipality must financially support entrepreneurs in making buildings with a public function accessible. Together, we work towards an inclusive city where everyone feels welcome.

115000.explanation: Local democracy also requires a strong local broadcaster. In exceptional situations, such as with Haarlem105, extra support is needed. Therefore, additional funds have already been made available for the near future.

115003.explanation: Your Haarlem finds sports clubs indispensable for the city. The primary responsibility lies with the clubs themselves, but if they get into trouble despite that, the municipality must provide financial assistance where necessary. This way, both the club and the sport are preserved.

115006.explanation: Your Haarlem finds safety important but is cautious about camera surveillance. It can sometimes help in specific locations, but only as a supplement to other measures. Privacy remains paramount, so we opt for tailored solutions.

115007.explanation: Your Haarlem believes that enforcement officers should be able to do their work safely. That is why we are cautious about batons: these can increase tensions and escalate situations. We choose good training, clear tasks, and strong cooperation with the police as a safer approach.

115008.explanation: Your Haarlem believes that residents of Haarlem should have a fair chance at housing in their own city. The law allows up to half of social rental homes to be allocated to local housing seekers, and we want to make use of that possibility.

115011.explanation: Your Haarlem has advocated for years for flexible housing for young people and students. Temporary housing, such as container homes, can be built quickly and nowadays is sustainable, comfortable, and of excellent quality. The municipality must invest in this to address the housing shortage.

115012.explanation: Your Haarlem believes in reciprocity with perspective. Participation is important, but socially meaningful work must fit a person's situation and abilities. No coercion, but support and opportunities to contribute to society and improve oneself.

115013.explanation: Your Haarlem believes that people with low incomes should not be pushed further into trouble. Waiving municipal taxes prevents debts and stress. Ending the waiver would actually increase inequality.

115014.explanation: Your Haarlem believes more people should be able to receive help with poverty. Due to sharply rising prices, more and more residents of Haarlem are struggling. Therefore, we want to raise the income threshold to 150% of the social minimum so that more people gain financial security.

115017.explanation: Your Haarlem believes charging stations are necessary but not at the expense of parking spaces. Only if the number of electric cars increases significantly can extra charging points be added. Parking spaces must be preserved for people without electric cars.

115021.explanation: Your Haarlem believes that good and accessible care for everyone is paramount. Where possible, there should be room for care that fits a person's background, but that is not always possible.

115024.explanation: Jouw Haarlem believes that the dog tax is outdated. It is unfair and requires a lot of effort to monitor who owns a dog and who doesn't. The municipality can better spend that time on other matters.

115026.explanation: Jouw Haarlem wants to preserve and strengthen existing employment. We aim to bring work and housing closer together and make better use of existing business parks, for example through business collective buildings. Because space is scarce, we focus on smart use of what already exists.

115031.explanation: Jouw Haarlem believes the current number of coffeeshops is sufficient. Haarlem has 16 and clear agreements have been made about this number. It will not increase any further. This provides peace and clarity for residents, entrepreneurs, and enforcement. We do support the relocation of Lounge to the Waarderpolder.

115032.explanation: Jouw Haarlem sees that in recent years a significant backlog has developed in the construction of social rental housing. The number of homes owned by housing corporations has even decreased. To catch up on this backlog, we want at least half of new constructions to consist of social rental homes.

115035.explanation: Jouw Haarlem believes in the power of involved citizens. In the Neighborhood House, residents meet each other and together ensure a safe, livable, and connected neighborhood. We want to expand this to every district.

115037.explanation: Preserving existing parking spaces is already challenging enough, and expansion is often expensive or impossible. Therefore, we want to maintain parking spots in existing neighborhoods and ensure sufficient parking spaces in new developments.

115038.explanation: Jouw Haarlem wants a lively and accessible city center. Fewer cars can help, but a completely car-free center is not workable for everyone. We choose a tailored approach: space for pedestrians and cyclists, with good accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, and emergency services.

115039.explanation: Jouw Haarlem opposes making the west side of the Spaarne river low-traffic. Closing it would make the De Appelaar parking garage inaccessible and make it harder to exit the city. This would lock down that part of the city, which we do not want.

115040.explanation: Jouw Haarlem wants heat networks to eventually be fully sustainable. This must be done carefully, with residents' support. Heat networks must be affordable and proven effective. We do not want fossil-fuel-based networks, but also not mandatory solutions without clear evidence that they work well.

115041.explanation: Jouw Haarlem believes paid parking may only be introduced if a majority of neighborhood residents want it. We want to reverse the decision by PvdA, GroenLinks, D66, and CDA to implement this in seven districts so residents can decide for themselves again.

114986.explanation: It is not about gender but about good governance.

114987.explanation: Management of public spaces and buildings is a core task of the municipality.

114989.explanation: We believe some regulation is necessary to prevent nuisance for neighbors.

114990.explanation: There must always be shelter for genuine refugees. The law also imposes obligations on the municipality, but that does not change that we are against a permanent asylum center in Haarlem.

114991.explanation: Asylum seekers were rejected for good reason; they are here illegally and must leave.

114993.explanation: The municipality already interferes enough in our lives.

114994.explanation: Hospitality businesses should decide for themselves whether they want to make this investment.

114995.explanation: This is a good alternative to a fireworks ban combined with stricter enforcement. The municipality facilitates. The fireworks show is based on private initiative, including funding.

114998.explanation: This is not a municipal task, but the responsibility of the entrepreneur.

115000.explanation: Haarlem 105 rightfully also receives additional subsidies.

115003.explanation: There is already a lot of subsidy going to sports fields and buildings. If there are difficulties, an association should in principle solve them itself.

115006.explanation: Given international developments, we are against this here. But due to lack of enforcement, an exception must be possible, for example at Stationsplein.

115007.explanation: The public order officers already have a hard enough time carrying out their duties.

115011.explanation: There is an urgent shortage of youth housing in the short term, and container homes can provide an immediate solution.

115012.explanation: Everyone must make a meaningful contribution to society, also for the sake of their own well-being.

115013.explanation: Currently, the waste disposal levy is paid by other residents without this being clear. We also want to raise awareness.

115014.explanation: In this way, working pays less and less, and the municipality already provides maximum support.

115017.explanation: But only if the neighborhood requests it; it should not be a way to remove parking spaces, but to facilitate car usage.

115021.explanation: Care should be universal, and we should be grateful when someone comes.

115024.explanation: The dog tax serves only to fill the municipal treasury.

115026.explanation: There is nothing to expand in the most built-up city in the Netherlands.

115031.explanation: There are enough coffeeshops in Haarlem.

115032.explanation: Some projects in Haarlem are already 100% social. Apartment blocks are getting taller, smaller, and closer together. At the same time, there is no flow from existing social housing because insufficient housing is built for the middle groups.

115035.explanation: Not every district needs an expensive community center. In practice, there are already enough existing rooms available in club buildings, cultural centers, hospitality venues, churches, etc.

115037.explanation: The policy now is that parking spaces are only being removed. As long as there is no alternative to the car, there must be enough parking spaces.

115038.explanation: Living in the city center must be attractive and therefore remain accessible by car.

115039.explanation: Enough measures are already being taken to keep cars out of the city. The Appelaar and Kamp parking garages must also remain accessible.

115040.explanation: Plans must be feasible and not based on extreme climate ideals.

114986.explanation: The Action Party considers it important that the city administration is representative of the residents. Besides an equal number of men and women, this also includes representation of people who do not identify with a gender and those from diverse cultural backgrounds.

114987.explanation: The Action Party values a positive attitude from the government towards initiatives by residents. Engagement with and influence over one's own living environment fosters mutual connection. Participation and the "right to challenge" are inseparably linked to governing the city.

114989.explanation: The Action Party advocates for extended opening hours in the hospitality sector. This makes the city attractive to many people. Of course, we want good agreements to be made regarding social safety (at night) and with nearby residents.

114990.explanation: Haarlem is a welcoming city. The Action Party wants good shelter and facilities for people fleeing war and violence. We want to meet the obligations of the Distribution Act and support volunteers who help newcomers get started in Haarlem.

114991.explanation: We do not want people to disappear into illegality. Therefore, it is important that undocumented individuals are temporarily housed, for example in municipal properties. There are social organizations involved in sheltering these people. They deserve full support from the municipality.

114993.explanation: The Action Party supports clean air. The executive board has proposed a phased approach, starting with education and the use of the Stookwijzer (heating guide). This tool shows everyone whether using wood burning is inadvisable due to weather conditions. The Action Party supports this approach.

114994.explanation: The Action Party favors phasing out environmentally harmful terrace heaters. There are good alternatives, such as electric terrace cushions. Subsidies for these can be linked to agreements with hospitality entrepreneurs to remove existing heaters.

114995.explanation: The Action Party supports clean air. Fireworks are very polluting, and we consider it a bad signal if the government itself sets off fireworks. Good alternatives can be found with light shows and similar events.

114998.explanation: The Action Party finds building accessibility important. However, we believe this should primarily be the responsibility of the building owner. Therefore, the Action Party invests in the accessibility of its own government buildings, including the city hall and public service offices, as well as other municipal properties.

115000.explanation: The Action Party considers it important that there is a broad range of independent local media in Haarlem. Recently, the Action Party strongly advocated for support for Haarlem105. Where possible, we want the municipality of Haarlem to purchase advertising space in local media for multiple years.

115003.explanation: The Action Party supports grassroots sports. We invest in outdoor exercise opportunities, a river swimming pool in the Spaarne, and accessibility of sports facilities. We invest in two swimming pools, De Houtvaart, a gymnastics hall, and gymnasiums. Sports clubs themselves work towards healthy financial operations.

115006.explanation: The Action Party is not in favor of constant and everywhere camera surveillance in public spaces. Temporary use of cameras in certain locations can have a deterrent effect. Cameras bring privacy risks. We want to prevent recorded images from being stored indefinitely.

115007.explanation: The Action Party believes that enforcement officers must be able to work safely. Their tasks should be adjusted accordingly. After a trial, we have not seen scientific evidence supporting the use of batons. If an evaluation again proves they have no added value, we advocate for their abolition once more.

115008.explanation: In 2026, various priority arrangements will be developed. For example, for cohabitants who leave a social rental home, for affordable home purchases, or for people working in education or healthcare. The Action Party supports this.

115011.explanation: The Action Party finds it important that young people can develop themselves and build their own lives. 'Moving out' is a natural step. Besides room rental (hospita) and building (temporary) student housing, we would also like to enable collective living arrangements.

115012.explanation: For those who (temporarily) do not participate in the labor market, there must be a good social safety net. We prefer positive incentives over sanctions. The starting point for labor market integration should be based on what a person wants and can do. Sufficient opportunities for (re)training are important.

115013.explanation: This intention leads to more debts among residents, human suffering, and higher costs.

115014.explanation: In 2024, the Participation Council advised to extend the income limit of 130% to 140% in urgent cases. The Action Party wants to investigate these possibilities and will seize any opportunity to expand offers for Haarlempas holders.

115017.explanation: The Action Party supports the mobility transition to cleaner means of transport. Network congestion affects many people, and we want to prevent that problem from getting worse through more charging stations. We do what we can, but in case of scarcity, we prioritize the greatest societal benefit.

115021.explanation: We regard individual freedom of choice as a great good, especially concerning people's health or wellbeing. However, given the tight labor market, it is questionable whether a real choice exists in all cases.

115024.explanation: The revenue from dog taxes can be used for 'dog stops' with poop bags and improved distribution of trash bins throughout the city.

115026.explanation: Space in the city is limited. Adding extra floors on existing business premises, for example in the Waarderpolder area, is possible.

115031.explanation: The current number of coffeeshops is sufficient. However, their distribution can be improved. We consider Haarlem's quality mark for coffeeshops a positive development.

115032.explanation: The Action Party opts for 100% social and affordable housing. This means we no longer want construction of (expensive) private sector homes in new plans. The requirement of 40% social housing is an absolute minimum. Citywide, on both sides of the Spaarne, we strive for 50% social housing where possible.

115035.explanation: In a social city, there is room for meeting others. There are excellent examples of neighborhood centers where great work is done. We find it very important that community centers run by the neighborhood itself continue to exist and inspire others to start similar initiatives. We wish every neighborhood a Community Hub.

115037.explanation: We believe cars take up too much scarce public space. The Action Party wants parking revenues to be saved in a mobility fund for constructing parking garages. We support parking on private property and shared use of parking lots by businesses and residents.

115038.explanation: With the expansion of the low-traffic city center, we want to simultaneously guarantee accessibility for people with mobility impairments. First, we see parked cars on Groot- and Klein Heiligland moved to De Kamp.

115039.explanation: Ideally, we want through traffic along the Spaarne to be redirected already at the Catharijnebrug. The De Appelaar parking garage remains accessible for the court and theater visitors, among others. This will create more space for pedestrians along the most beautiful part of the Spaarne.

115040.explanation: We want to be off natural gas by 2040. The Action Party supports a social energy transition. Residents will be informed 10 years before the heating network is realized. For necessary interim replacements, the Action Party wants to offer residents good options for leasing or renting boilers.

115041.explanation: We want to focus on introducing parking regulations in the 7 neighborhoods where agreements have been made. We invest in parking solutions. We advocate citywide parking regulation and establish arrangements for people with disabilities, healthcare providers, visitors, and parking permit caps.

114986.explanation: Each coalition party is responsible for nominating its own aldermen. It is good to have both men and women in the executive board, but we will not fix an equal number. Incidentally, the majority of the current municipal council members are women.

114987.explanation: Definitely a good thing to do. This can also apply to, for example, community centers. However, good agreements are needed so that the municipality knows what it can expect from residents and vice versa.

114989.explanation: The possibilities for opening hours have recently been extended and this has been supported by all parties. Thus, the opening hours are sufficiently generous in our view. Protect neighbors against nuisance.

114990.explanation: We comply with legal obligations for asylum seeker shelter and ensure sufficient activities together with volunteer organizations. We tackle nuisance. We assist undocumented people and those with rejected asylum claims in obtaining the correct papers and returning.

114991.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers must return. If that is not possible, they risk becoming victims of exploitation and abuse. We want to prevent this. If countries of origin do not cooperate sufficiently with return, rejected asylum seekers should not be the ones to pay the price!

114993.explanation: Modern wood stoves are already quite clean. Moreover, a ban is hardly enforceable. A municipal recommendation not to burn in calm weather is fine. Provide good information on efficient burning and encourage the installation of wood smoke filters.

114994.explanation: Terrace heaters must be banned. As long as this is not legally possible, the municipality should make agreements with the Royal Dutch Hospitality Association to avoid using terrace heaters in Haarlem.

114995.explanation: Now that there is a ban on setting off fireworks, shows are a good alternative. However, organizing these is not a government task. The municipality can facilitate such shows by granting permits. Our preference is for a drone show because it is better for the environment.

114998.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for their buildings, but interventions can sometimes be costly. A subsidy can help then. The municipality should also physically remove thresholds by adjusting sidewalks. This way, many thresholds at shop entrances in the city center can be eliminated.

115000.explanation: Additional funding is needed so Haarlem105 can continue to carry out its social and journalistic mission professionally. A prerequisite is that other municipalities in the region also contribute. We will also continue with the media fund to subsidize local journalistic projects.

115003.explanation: Associations must be self-sufficient. The municipality ensures proper maintenance of sports fields and provides subsidies to sports clubs for their facilities. Clubs reasonably contribute to the operation and upkeep of these facilities.

115006.explanation: Cameras can increase safety or the perception of safety. But we want to handle this very cautiously and carefully due to privacy concerns. We prefer temporary use where needed rather than permanent installation!

115007.explanation: Society has changed and the role of enforcement officers has become more demanding. We trust the professionalism of enforcement officers when using batons. The municipality, as an employer, is obligated to protect enforcement officers and give them the means to perform their job properly.

115008.explanation: By law, priority can be given for 50% of the homes. We use this to give Haarlem house seekers a better chance at a home. But we also need to build much more for all target groups.

115011.explanation: Partly agree, but it is better to ensure permanent housing. Haarlem needs more student housing. We must build a lot more!

115012.explanation: Our principle is: Everyone participates and is supported where necessary to contribute to society. This can also include volunteer work. People are usually on welfare for a reason. Of course, someone who genuinely cannot work is exempt.

115013.explanation: People with low incomes already often struggle to make ends meet financially. Abolishing waivers for municipal taxes will cause many people to fall into debt. Such debts cost the municipality much more money.

115014.explanation: Haarlem has generous schemes for people with low incomes. Too many of them are not reached. We specifically want to help these people. We also want not only children but adults with low incomes to be able to play sports affordably.

115017.explanation: The number of charging points must grow along with the number of electric cars. Thanks to the Christian Union, residents can now also charge their car from home with a cable mat. This reduces the need for extra charging poles. We also want cable ducts to be allowed.

115021.explanation: We stand for freedom of choice. Care based on your own faith, culture, or beliefs fits with this. We also want more room for the use of personal budgets. Of course, care must comply with all legal requirements.

115024.explanation: Abolishing dog tax means another tax must go up. However, we want to lower the dog tax for the first dog. To encourage cleaning up dog poop and litter, we will place 500 extra waste bins in the city.

115026.explanation: There is no room for extra industrial estates. We must use space more cleverly. There is still a lot of space in the Waarderpolder if we build higher: four instead of one floor. This also creates room for extra housing along the Haarlem to Amsterdam railway.

115031.explanation: We want fewer coffeeshops. The minimum distance to schools must be increased from 150 to 250 meters.

115032.explanation: The housing shortage is severe. This applies not only to social rental (waiting time over 8 years!) but also to mid-range rentals and home ownership. Percentages don’t put roofs over heads. We must simply build more for all target groups! By the way, we already build 40% social housing in Haarlem.

115035.explanation: Most neighborhoods have a community center. We are not aware of any shortage of space anywhere. Now that many homes are being added, amenities must of course grow accordingly. So, some additional community centers will be needed.

115037.explanation: In some neighborhoods there are more cars than parking spaces. However, there is usually no space for additional places. Building parking garages is expensive. We did support the construction of a new parking garage in the Indische Buurt.

115038.explanation: A large part of the city center is already car-free. We can imagine adding a few more streets if supported by the residents. However, making the entire center car-free is unrealistic.

115039.explanation: We want to investigate whether the area south of the garage can be made car-free. Cars must still be able to access the parking garage de Appelaar. We also want to prevent unnecessary traffic by encouraging visitors to park elsewhere. Space in the garage would then be reserved for residents.

115040.explanation: A district heating network is intended as a sustainable heat supply. We are therefore against new heating networks powered by gas or other fossil fuels. Furthermore, affordability for residents is a firm requirement. Heat pumps are sometimes more sustainable and cheaper.

115041.explanation: Paid parking only applies in neighborhoods where parking pressure is too high. Parking is not a source of revenue. It is about the neighborhood’s livability. Costs for the first parking permit for residents should be reduced.

114986.explanation: Equality is not a mandatory quota. Leaders should be chosen based on quality, experience, and vision, not based on gender.

114987.explanation: Management of public facilities belongs to the municipality. Burdening residents with government tasks is not a solution, but shirking responsibility.

114989.explanation: BVNL stands for freedom and entrepreneurship. Hospitality entrepreneurs know best when their customers come. The government should not interfere with closing times.

114990.explanation: Our municipality must first care for its own residents. The reception of asylum seekers places too much pressure on housing, amenities, and safety.

114991.explanation: Those who have been rejected must leave the country. Haarlem is not a reception center for people without residency rights.

114993.explanation: People should be able to decide how they heat their homes. Banning open fireplaces and wood stoves is nannying and does not fit a free society.

114994.explanation: Entrepreneurs should decide for themselves whether to use terrace heating. Banning it is nannying and bad for hospitality businesses.

114995.explanation: A municipal fireworks show costs a lot of money and spoils the fun for people. BVNL favors personal fireworks: own responsibility, own party.

114998.explanation: Accessibility is important, but responsibility lies with the entrepreneur themselves. Public money should not be used for adaptations to private property.

115000.explanation: A strong local broadcaster is important for democratic oversight and citizen engagement. Extra support is justified if it operates independently and professionally.

115003.explanation: Sports clubs play an important role in society. Temporary support can help keep them afloat in difficult times.

115006.explanation: Camera surveillance helps in catching criminals and increases safety. But it must respect privacy and common sense.

115007.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers must be able to defend themselves when the situation requires it. A baton increases their safety and authority on the street.

115008.explanation: First our own residents get a home. Social housing is intended for people who live here and contribute to the community.

115011.explanation: Temporary solutions do not address the structural housing shortage. The focus must be on permanent affordable housing, without wasting money.

115012.explanation: Those who can work must contribute. Benefits are not free income but should be linked to responsibility.

115013.explanation: Waivers are unfair to people who work hard but are just above the threshold. Those who can pay should simply pay.

115014.explanation: Constantly raising the limits makes more people dependent on the government. Help should go to those who really need it, not to everyone who asks for it.

115017.explanation: Electric driving is a choice, not a right. Sacrificing regular parking spaces for charging poles is unfair to other road users.

115021.explanation: Care is about quality, not ideological preference. No one may make demands based on faith or culture about who helps them.

115024.explanation: Dog tax is an outdated and unfair charge.

115026.explanation: Business parks provide jobs and economic growth. Haarlem should make room for entrepreneurs who want to invest and expand.

115031.explanation: New coffeeshops can help spread out demand and reduce nuisance.

115032.explanation: A mandatory 50% social housing quota slows construction and drives away investors. Haarlem actually needs more mid-rent and affordable owner-occupied homes to get the housing market going.

115035.explanation: Not every neighborhood needs a community center. Expensive buildings do not solve social problems and should not be an end in themselves.
