115037.explanation: In many neighborhoods, parking space is a daily problem. More parking spots mean less stress and better livability.

115038.explanation: A car-free city center makes the city inaccessible for entrepreneurs, the elderly, and people with disabilities. Accessibility is not a luxury but a necessity.

115039.explanation: More traffic restrictions simply push the problem to other streets. Good traffic flow and accessibility must come first.

115040.explanation: Compulsory 'completely sustainable' heating networks exclude affordable and reliable solutions. Let innovation and freedom of choice lead, not ideology.

115041.explanation: If residents want it, parking should be free again in their neighborhood. The municipality should listen to the community and not impose paid parking unilaterally.

114986.explanation: FVD selects people based on their qualities, not their gender.

114987.explanation: If there is a good plan, residents should have the freedom to take care of something in their community. This is not possible for all facilities, but custom solutions where feasible should be allowed.

114989.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have the freedom to regulate this properly themselves to do the best for their business. Rights come with responsibilities, so it is also up to them to manage this well. If there is too much nuisance, corrections can be made.

114990.explanation: FVD does not want reception centers in Haarlem to ensure safety and relieve pressure on our facilities.

114991.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers stay here illegally; they should return to their country of origin.

114993.explanation: FVD supports residents' freedom to decide how they want to heat their homes, whether with a cozy fireplace, gas heating, or a heat pump.

114994.explanation: Terrace heating makes it pleasant to sit outside at a hospitality venue when it's cold. Banning this has serious consequences for entrepreneurs.

114995.explanation: The municipality may organize this if there is interest. Although a large municipal fireworks show is a nice idea, it should not come at the expense of the ability to set off your own fireworks on New Year's Eve. We cherish the tradition of setting off our own fireworks.

114998.explanation: This is the entrepreneur’s own choice. It should not be funded with municipal money.

115000.explanation: Our local broadcaster receives sufficient funding. If they fall short, they must make choices like any other company.

115003.explanation: Sports clubs play an important role in our society. It should be carefully considered whether a problem is incidental or structural. With a good follow-up plan, the municipality could provide temporary support.

115006.explanation: We deploy cameras incidentally and temporarily at targeted locations. This should not lead to large-scale surveillance.

115007.explanation: This creates a false sense of security. We keep enforcement officers (BOA's) to their core duties and do not turn them into an alternative police force. The police are better trained for certain powers.

115008.explanation: FVD wants residents of Haarlem and their children to have first priority for social housing and affordable homeownership.

115011.explanation: Haarlem has a shortage of student and youth housing. Where possible, we should improve this.

115012.explanation: A counter-performance may be required from people on welfare benefits who can work (to some extent). They also have a place and a contribution to make to our society.

115013.explanation: This is a matter of customization. In special cases, this should remain possible. We should not do this on a structural basis.

115014.explanation: Haarlem has many minimum income schemes up to 130% of the minimum wage, such as the Haarlempas. Further raising the income threshold for social policy only makes working less rewarding.

115017.explanation: You must carefully consider the need. A charging spot for electric cars also means a priority spot for these cars, which removes a regular parking space.

115021.explanation: FVD stands for freedom of choice. Where possible, preferences can be taken into account.

115024.explanation: A dog is a friend, not a financial milking cow. Haarlem is one of the last cities that still charges this tax. Dog owners get nothing in return. FVD supports abolishing this tax.

115026.explanation: There is not much space in the entire city to increase the total area of business parks, but if entrepreneurs have a logical plan or want to take over another site, that option should certainly be available.

115031.explanation: Provided no nuisance is experienced, yes. Entrepreneurs should not be restricted in this, it is up to the market. However, we think this market in Haarlem is already quite saturated.

115032.explanation: FVD supports a maximum of 30% social housing. We want a rental market where supply and demand match. With sufficient room for private rental. We focus on middle incomes, families, and starters. Social housing should remain a safety net for those who need it.

115035.explanation: A community center can be a good way to connect the neighborhood.

115037.explanation: FVD wants more underground parking garages, but at the very least, the current number of parking spaces should be maintained.

115038.explanation: A large part of the city center is already car-free or low-traffic. That is nice for shopping and terraces. Outside this area, the city must remain accessible; we therefore see no added value in making an even larger area car-free.

115039.explanation: This area is already low-traffic, not car-free. We want to keep it that way; the city must remain accessible.

115040.explanation: FVD does not want to phase out gas. We maintain this clean fuel as a stable and reliable energy source. Moreover, residents of Haarlem decide for themselves how to heat their homes.

115041.explanation: For neighborhoods where this is logical, yes. FVD supports customization and support measurements. When a neighborhood largely believes that paid parking is logical and necessary-such as in the center-it can be introduced there. In other areas, however, it is entirely unwanted and unnecessary.

114986.explanation: Volt fundamentally strives for gender parity. Therefore, if possible, this should also apply to the college of mayor and aldermen.

114987.explanation: Volt supports citizen participation wherever possible. This includes forms of self-governance, provided continuity can be ensured.

114989.explanation: Fixed opening and closing times provide predictability and quiet in the city. Exceptions can be made for nighttime venues.

114990.explanation: Volt advocates for a generous reception of asylum seekers and their swift integration into society and the labor market. This reduces the demand for labor migrants.

114991.explanation: For rejected asylum seekers, at least a bed-bath-bread facility must remain available until their departure.

114993.explanation: Wood burning will naturally decrease as homes become well insulated and modernly heated, but it may still be necessary for the time being.

114994.explanation: Hospitality entrepreneurs should be encouraged to adopt sustainable practices. They should decide themselves where to set their priorities.

114995.explanation: Fireworks remain dangerous and polluting. However, the municipality should support alternative forms of New Year's celebrations.

114998.explanation: Volt believes there should be no restrictions on entering public or private buildings. If an entrance from the public space poses a problem, the municipality also has a responsibility here.

115000.explanation: Volt believes that in a full-fledged democracy local media should be supported with sufficient funding or publicly paid content to inform residents neutrally.

115003.explanation: All associations are fundamentally responsible for their own continuity. The municipality supports the construction and maintenance of facilities.

115006.explanation: According to various studies, camera surveillance increases the feeling of insecurity on the street and infringes on citizens' privacy. Exceptions can be made for combating scooter and moped nuisance in pedestrian and busy entertainment areas, and at certain events.

115007.explanation: Law enforcement officers and supervisors must feel safe on the street. A condition is that users of batons receive adequate training in self-defense and violence control.

115008.explanation: Besides general agreements concerning special groups such as people with social or medical indications or residence permit holders, Volt advocates giving priority housing in Haarlem to people who work or study there, regardless of their origin.

115011.explanation: Student housing can be realized in existing buildings and homes by relaxing regulations (e.g., landlord presence, house sharing).

115012.explanation: Volt supports a basic income for every citizen without requiring any performance in return. Pilot projects in Finland and Oregon (USA) have proven very successful. Social assistance could then be abolished.

115013.explanation: Financial stress is a major cause of social, psychological, and physical problems among the elderly and youth. It is more humane and cheaper to prevent this stress than to solve social and health problems afterward.

115014.explanation: Financial stress is a major cause of social, psychological, and physical problems among the elderly and youth. It is more humane and cheaper to prevent this stress than to solve social and health problems afterward.

115017.explanation: The shift to electric driving requires a gradual increase in the number of parking spaces equipped with charging stations.

115021.explanation: Healthcare is a public service offered without any form of distinction based on people's cultural backgrounds.

115024.explanation: The dog tax is an outdated levy that no longer relates to its original purpose: preventing diseases. Volt therefore supports its abolition, with compensation of the net proceeds in other general levies.

115026.explanation: Haarlem no longer has space for significant expansion of industrial areas.

115031.explanation: Volt believes the coffee shop policy should be liberalized. This means the same norms and permits should apply to the entire sector.

115032.explanation: The share in Haarlem is already above the national average at 40%. Affordable housing can be created faster and cheaper by dividing, splitting, and mixing living and working in existing buildings.

115035.explanation: Neighborhood centers are accessible cultural and social meeting places that play a crucial role in promoting and preserving social cohesion in a district; this is especially true in neighborhoods with a culturally diverse population.

115037.explanation: Research shows there are sufficient parking spaces for residents in the neighborhoods. Volt supports reducing the role of the car in our mobility.

115038.explanation: Volt supports expanding the car-free area in the city center. However, the inner city must remain livable and accessible for residents and entrepreneurs. This means you should be able to reach your doorstep by car.

115039.explanation: Volt supports expanding the car-free area in the city center. However, the inner city must remain livable and accessible for residents and entrepreneurs. This means you should be able to reach your doorstep by car.

115040.explanation: Due to the lack of sustainable heat sources in Haarlem, building large heating networks is pointless. Another approach is needed. Volt wants to replace the current district-focused approach with a building-focused one, making every building gas-free within the next 15-25 years.

115041.explanation: Volt believes that parking should eventually be paid throughout the city, with rates varying by neighborhood.

114986.explanation: The college of mayor and aldermen should consist of the most capable and motivated people. More women would be welcome, but no more than that as this statement reads.

114987.explanation: The 'right-to-challenge' does not exist if controllability is lost. When residents take over management, the same requirements apply to implementation.

114991.explanation: To prevent 'disappearing' into illegality, it is better to offer shelter also to rejected asylum seekers. Whether this is a regional function of the city, i.e., providing shelter, is still in question.

114993.explanation: On this, the Pirate Party owes you a motivation.

114994.explanation: Terrace heating comes in many forms, but until there is one that operates completely neutrally

114995.explanation: The Pirate Party wants an efficient government. Professional fireworks shows can also be organized individually or jointly under the umbrella of a neighborhood platform.

114998.explanation: Should the municipality or the UWV be responsible for the accessibility of a location for a visitor to a business? We foresee many pitfalls.

115000.explanation: Access to all cultural events. At least all events that receive subsidies can be more broadly accessed through the presence of a local broadcaster.

115003.explanation: Pirate Party Haarlem considers sports important, and financial independence healthy. Through programs such as the Haarlempas, people who otherwise cannot afford sports club membership can still participate with support from the municipality. Then a good budget and member recruitment are the responsibilities of the club.

115006.explanation: Not only turn off existing cameras but remove them.

115007.explanation: Why should we equip a BOA with even more training for the safe use of a baton? No! The use of force is reserved for the 'executive branch': The Police! If this granting of resources continues, we will soon have the same costs for a new 'light' police force.

115008.explanation: The Pirate Party remains indebted to you for motivation on this.

115011.explanation: Is there a long-term vision on the disappearance of students? No. Then temporary housing is not the solution.

115012.explanation: Sorry, but what are we talking about? A person is completely down and out and then someone else decides what that person should do? Get lost! 'socially meaningful'.

115013.explanation: With a guaranteed unconditional basic income, there would no longer be any reason for a scheme like this; the idea of a low income would disappear.

115014.explanation: The minimum wage is too low to make ends meet; maintaining a percentage of that minimum wage (120-150%) as an income limit is almost criminal.

115017.explanation: The municipality must come up with a comprehensive plan for an energy transition by 2030! And let residents install charging points themselves.

115021.explanation: For other reasons too, and even complete arbitrariness should be an option.

115026.explanation: It is important to expand business parks in ways that keep the landscape intact or further enrich it with more diversity.

115031.explanation: Mainly sales outlets that participate in the new chain of legal cultivation and quality control; suddenly the municipality has the possibility to levy a charge for the benefit of general funds.

115032.explanation: More or less, as long as we let people earn at or around the minimum, there will also be a need for housing that accommodates those people.

115035.explanation: Where there is need, this can be signaled by neighborhood platforms. Saying 'all neighborhoods' and 'must' is the wrong choice of words.

115037.explanation: Innovative mobility, modern electric shared cars are such a concept, fitting better with a modern city.

115038.explanation: The residents of Haarlem themselves decide which streets get other designations: such as which roads become bicycle streets or where road closures come to prevent shortcut traffic, etc.

115039.explanation: Here the Pirate Party sees opportunities for mixing traffic in a low-speed zone. It won’t be truly car-free as with a ban, but with pavement like a square or market, it immediately becomes clear that it is not a racetrack (as sometimes happens now).

115040.explanation: Methane generated from biomass is a gas. Hydrogen (H2) is as well.

115041.explanation: The neighborhood itself leads here, preferably in a dialogue among residents, stakeholders, and interested parties.

114986.explanation: Equal representation strengthens decision-making and the exemplary function of governance.

114987.explanation: Citizen initiatives are valuable, but only voluntary and with good support; no shifting of tasks.

114989.explanation: More space is possible, but with clear agreements on nuisance, safety, and enforcement.

114990.explanation: Shelter is a humanitarian duty. Closure solves nothing and increases problems.

114991.explanation: Nobody should end up on the street. Shelter prevents human suffering and nuisance.

114993.explanation: Wood burning harms air quality and health, especially in densely built neighborhoods.

114994.explanation: Waste of energy is unsustainable. Sustainable alternatives are possible.

114995.explanation: Provides safety, less nuisance, and a celebration for everyone.

114998.explanation: Accessibility is not a luxury but a basic right.

115000.explanation: Local journalism strengthens democracy and oversight.

115003.explanation: Sports clubs are the social heart of neighborhoods.

115006.explanation: Can help with specific problems, but privacy must be paramount.

115008.explanation: Local ties may count, without excluding others.

115011.explanation: Emergency solutions are needed as long as structural construction lags behind.

115012.explanation: Coercion backfires; guidance and trust work better.

115014.explanation: Many people currently just fall outside the schemes.

115017.explanation: Necessary for sustainability, but must not come at the expense of accessibility.

115021.explanation: Good care requires trust and recognition.

115024.explanation: The polluter should bear the costs if this causes many extra expenses for the municipality.

115026.explanation: Employment is important, but not at the expense of the living environment.

115031.explanation: Only if this contributes to dispersion and less nuisance.

115032.explanation: Without a strong standard, housing remains unaffordable.

115035.explanation: Meeting and social cohesion are crucial.

115037.explanation: Space is scarce; focus on smart solutions. At the same time, we will apply customized approaches.

115038.explanation: Car-free zones where possible, but accessibility remains important. Especially for entrepreneurs.

115040.explanation: No new dependence on fossil fuels.

114986.explanation: We just want the best people. So they could all be women. But start immediately with an elected mayor.

114987.explanation: A public garden seems a good idea in any case. Now the municipality talks a lot about greenery this and that, but when it comes to maintenance they don’t deliver. The residents who take over do receive a compensation.

114989.explanation: Not that they want to. The hospitality industry is happy with the current rules. Some can stay open until 5 a.m., and on a few days, after request, they may remain open until 6 a.m. This works well. And nuisance stays limited thanks to the calm team and various closing times. Of course, they should also be able to close earlier.

114990.explanation: Yes, because if the asylum reception is closed, it means there is no new influx and the current asylum seekers are well integrated and naturalized and no longer have a special status.

114991.explanation: Rejected is rejected. If you keep hosting them anyway, you undermine your own reception system.

114993.explanation: Clean burning is fine. Everything is becoming electric now and we have to get off gas. But with network congestion and external threats, it is already predicted that we could be without power for 72 hours. Then clean wood burning is the only backup way to keep warm.

114995.explanation: We are against a fireworks ban, but now that it exists, you have to be realistic and at least offer people an alternative.

114998.explanation: Then people must first do it themselves.

115000.explanation: We already do. A page from the municipality again in the local newspaper is also good to ensure people still receive some information from the municipality.

115003.explanation: See how it happened and decide specifically on a case-by-case basis.

115006.explanation: Only in nuisance areas as a temporary measure. We must prevent Haarlem from becoming a second China where everyone is monitored at all times of the day.

115007.explanation: They already have them. So yes. We must also think about their safety.

115008.explanation: Exactly the proposal of Trots Haarlem, but the major parties oppose it and Haarlemmers prefer to wait longer each year.

115011.explanation: Why only students? And don’t many students actually come from outside Haarlem?

115013.explanation: If they can’t afford it anyway and thus get into worse trouble, it’s nonsense. You only create more costs for society.

115014.explanation: People with two incomes hardly make ends meet anymore because of the absurd tax burden. The best is of course one low tax rate, so that working pays off. But until then, also help the working people alongside those on welfare.

115017.explanation: Definitely not if it costs existing parking spaces. They must be extra spaces. But can the electric grid handle it if every Haarlemmer trades their car in for an electric one? Probably not and it is therefore a disguised way to push people into poverty.

115021.explanation: The reality is that we should be glad people work in healthcare. Trots Haarlem is very grateful for that.

115024.explanation: Agreed. Our position for years. Haarlem is one of the very last municipalities to keep enforcing this, because it generates money.

115031.explanation: There are enough of them. Haarlem must not become a second Amsterdam.

115032.explanation: Haarlem has been applying 40-40-20 for years. And extra social housing is built where there is too little and less where there is already much more than that 40%. This way, no extra neighborhoods emerge that need attention.

115035.explanation: So true. Unbelievable that this still isn’t arranged by this social-left college.

115037.explanation: Only if additional construction takes place, enough parking spaces must be added for residents and visitors.

115038.explanation: Everyone must be able to get there without having to incur absurd costs or be endlessly on the road. Haarlem is there for the Haarlemmers.

115039.explanation: The municipality must especially watch that more and more people don’t suffer from transport poverty. If you are no longer allowed to enter the city center by car, are taxis also not allowed? And if you become fully dependent on taxis, how expensive will life actually become?

115040.explanation: If a district heating network has to be built at all. The aldermen have now become too dependent on the datacenter Iron Mountain, with possible undermining consequences or preferential positions, for example in paying sewage water charges.

115041.explanation: And that is possible according to Trots Haarlem’s plan. Everyone free in their own neighborhood.

Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
Haarlem should provide shelter for rejected asylum seekers.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should only allow district heating networks that are fully sustainable and no longer run on gas or other fossil fuels.
